topic: Program 5 (Craft/Home/Mobile) at 0% interest up to OMR 20K — who qualifies and how to apply
format: Open with a question about a business idea the reader has been sitting on, then show the exact first step to fund it
post:
You've had the idea for months. Maybe it's a home-based craft business. A mobile service. Something small but genuinely yours.

And it's still just an idea — because you don't know where to start with funding.

Here's what catches most people off guard: Development Bank Oman's Program 5 was built for exactly this. Craft, home, and mobile businesses. Up to OMR 20,000. Zero interest.

The basic requirements aren't intimidating — Omani national, viable idea, and a feasibility study that holds up to scrutiny.

That last part is where things break down. Not because the idea is bad. Because the document isn't structured the way DBO or Riyada actually expects it.

A Sanad office manager told me last month: "Most rejections I see aren't about the business. They're about the paperwork."

So before you book an appointment, before you dig out your CR documents — get your feasibility study right first. Understand your numbers. Know what questions are coming.

When the study is solid, the rest moves quickly.

Build a bank-ready feasibility study free in ~10 minutes — covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility

What's the one question you'd want answered before applying for DBO funding?
